description: This figure illustrates the experimental setup of a THz time-domain spectroscopy (THz-TDS) system. Part (a) shows a schematic of the THz beam path, including the Ti:Sapphire laser, beam splitters, lenses, THz emitter, mirrors (M1 and M2), sample position, and near-field THz detector. Part (b) is a microscopic image of the THz near-field detector electrodes fabricated on a silicon-on-sapphire substrate with a 5 µm gap.
key_findings: The THz beam is generated by a photoconductive emitter and directed via mirrors onto the sample and detector.
The near-field detector has microfabricated electrodes on a silicon-on-sapphire substrate with a 5 µm gap for high spatial resolution.
suggested_knowledge_points: THz beam generation and detection using photoconductive emitters and near-field detectors
